verdict: A place code stores far MORE environments than it has cells by high-dimensional superposition, exactly the polysemantic coding the intracranial recordings report (Elhage 2022). N cells recall ~4N sparse place fields (4x more environments than cells) where a monosemantic one-cell-per-place code could recall only N/F; each cell emerges polysemantic; and the compression collapses when activity is dense — so it is bought precisely by exploiting 'one place active at a time', not imposed.
